parsed_review.summary: CP00-646 is a descriptor-only RP21 Admin Console domain/service-bridge pack (150 units, risk C, range RP21.P01.M08.S06-RP21.P02.M07.S06) extending CP00-645. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level production_ready candidate (admin_console_domain_service_bridge_descriptor_verified) is supported and correctly scoped as a descriptor-verification claim, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim. (1) Implementation: packages/admin/src/{registry,service,validators,index}.js are the only source files; a Grep for writeFile/fs/child_process/network/process.env returned no matches — the code is pure frozen-object construction. freezeCp646Result pins all 26 runtime/write flags false (writes_product_state, executes_*_mutation_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_permission_decision, emits/persists_audit_event, create/update/delete DB rows, read/write object storage, opens_ui_runtime, exposes_blocked_claim_detail, persists_idempotency_key, persists_workflow_attempt, acquires_runtime_lock, leak_detected, permission_bypass_detected), and validateAdminConsoleCp646DomainServiceBridgeDescriptor enforces the identical false-set plus service_bridge runtime_handler_opened:false. (2) No-write/no-real-data: synthetic_only/no_real_data true and raw_payload_included false throughout; the validator script writes only behind --write, and npm rp21:admin-console:validate invokes it without --write (non-mutating). (3) Authority: claude_is_final_approver false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval false (validator-enforced), human_final_approval_required true, claude review packets read_only:true and blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2:true; manifest makes no enterprise-trust or runtime claim. (4) Hermes: gate-label H21 plus evidence descriptors with runtime_receipt_emitted:false and no_real_data:true — deterministic packet/receipt handling only, not embedded product runtime. (5) Live queue: plan live_state pack_id CP00-645 / next_subphase RP21.P01.M08.S06 equals CP00-646's first unit; CP00-645 (last unit RP21.P01.M08.S05) was just closed in git log, so CP00-646 is the contiguous live head; deviation_from_plan false, unit_count/range/distributions match and are asserted by model.test.js. (6) Validation evidence: command-evidence.json records 23 commands (node --check set, rp21 validators, npm test/validate, weighted/spec/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00 control-plane validate, build, git diff --check) all exit 0. (7) Review-evidence integrity (CP178+ hardening): closeout-pack-claude-review-hardening.mjs robustly detects auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON/prose, tool-call-shaped output, permission denials, and missing required fields, and closeout_eligible requires verdict PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS with blocks_pack_closeout/blocks_goal_closeout != true and p0=p1=p2=0; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1449B valid JSON); prompt_bytes 2739 < 25600; prompt_contains_full_diff_or_source false; forbidden_tools_present false; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. (8) Pre-promotion artifacts correctly avoid premature promotion: manifest in_progress/production_ready:false, construction-inspection PENDING_REVIEW, adjudication pending, claude-review-result pending_review with no accepted receipt. The prior attempt-01 was correctly held not-closeout-eligible for setting blocks_goal_closeout=true despite zero blocking findings; I independently confirmed its two P3 findings were inaccurate (the current baseline/prompt contain no rp05:matter-core:validate command, and the cp00-646 evidence dir is not double-classified as unrelated), so they are not carried forward. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two non-blocking P3 documentation/traceability observations remain. Pack closeout is not blocked; this CP closeout goal is not blocked (the pack makes no runtime/enterprise-trust claim, so the standing human-final-approval boundary does not block this receipt); Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not the final approval authority.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists the handoff doc in both the untracked-implementation and unrelated-preserved buckets
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 186) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 192). Inspecting the generator (scripts/generate-closeout-pack-claude-review-baseline.mjs) shows this is by design: untracked_implementation_files is the set of untracked files matching the broad isImplementationLikePath net (which includes docs/closeout-pack-plan/), while unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is the set of dirty files that are not active for this pack (not in manifest source_refs). new-session-handoff.md is genuinely not pack-active (absent from active_pack_implementation_refs and active_pack_required_untracked_files), so it legitimately satisfies both predicates. The cp00-646 evidence dir is correctly NOT in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. This is cosmetic and does not affect review scope: the operative safety gate active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no pack-active implementation file was excluded from review.
findings.recommendation: Optionally annotate untracked_implementation_files as an awareness superset, or make the two buckets mutually exclusive, to avoid the appearance of contradictory classification. Non-blocking — does not affect the production_ready determination.

findings.title: Preserved not-closeout-eligible attempt-01 is not referenced from the manifest/review-result attempt lists
findings.details: A prior valid-but-not-closeout-eligible review (attempt-01, blocks_goal_closeout=true with p0=p1=p2=0) is preserved on disk at artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-646/not-closeout-eligible-attempt-01.json (and raw-output-attempt-01-not-closeout-eligible.json) and acknowledged in command-evidence.json notes, but manifest pack_level_claude_review.invalid_review_attempts (line 5976) and claude-review-result.json invalid_review_attempts are both empty []. This is defensible because attempt-01 was valid_review (syntactically valid, not an 'invalid' attempt) and is tracked under a dedicated not-accepted record schema, but a reader of only the manifest would not see that a prior attempt occurred. Minor audit-traceability gap; the current review-receipt.json on disk remains closeout_eligible:false, so the pre-promotion state is safe.
findings.recommendation: Optionally surface the preserved not-closeout-eligible attempt in the manifest/claude-review-result attempt history for full audit traceability. Non-blocking — does not affect the production_ready determination.
